answer:
## `httpx.Client.get` — Signature & Arguments

Defined at `httpx/_client.py:1036`:

```python
def get(
    self,
    url: URL | str,
    *,
    params: QueryParamTypes | None = None,
    headers: HeaderTypes | None = None,
    cookies: CookieTypes | None = None,
    auth: AuthTypes | UseClientDefault | None = USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT,
    follow_redirects: bool | UseClientDefault = USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT,
    timeout: TimeoutTypes | UseClientDefault = USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT,
    extensions: RequestExtensions | None = None,
) -> Response:
```

### Arguments

| Argument | Type | Default | Position |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `URL \| str` | *(required)* | positional |
| `params` | `QueryParamTypes \| None` | `None` | keyword-only |
| `headers` | `HeaderTypes \| None` | `None` | keyword-only |
| `cookies` | `CookieTypes \| None` | `None` | keyword-only |
| `auth` | `AuthTypes \| UseClientDefault \| None` | `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` | keyword-only |
| `follow_redirects` | `bool \| UseClientDefault` | `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` | keyword-only |
| `timeout` | `TimeoutTypes \| UseClientDefault` | `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` | keyword-only |
| `extensions` | `RequestExtensions \| None` | `None` | keyword-only |

Returns: `Response`.

### Notes from architectural intent (ADR-01)

- Only `url` is positional; everything after `*` is **keyword-only**.
- The `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` sentinel means "fall back to the value configured on the `Client` instance." Passing `None` for `auth` means "explicitly disable auth" — the two are not the same. Users are encouraged to **omit** these parameters rather than pass `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` directly (it's an internal sentinel).
- `get` has no `content` / `data` / `files` / `json` parameters — bodies aren't part of GET. If you need to send a body, use `Client.request("GET", ...)` or `Client.post(...)` etc.
- The method just delegates to `self.request("GET", url, ...)`, so detailed parameter docs live on `httpx.request`.
- `AsyncClient.get` (line 1751) has a parallel signature with identical semantics, only differing in being `async def` and returning `Awaitable[Response]`.

ERR not_found
  MESSAGE Symbol 'Client.get' not found. Try find_by_intent if you're searching by concept rather than name.

SYM Client@httpx/_client.py:594 class
  SIG class Client(BaseClient)
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Auth objects must expose both sync_auth_flow and async_auth_flow adapting a shared generator-based auth_flow."
    RATIONALE "Auth is shared config on BaseClient but exercised by both sync and async I/O paths."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "All shared configuration (auth, headers, cookies, timeouts, redirect policy, event hooks, base URL, trust-env, encoding, lifecycle state) is held on BaseClient."
    RATIONALE "Eliminates duplication of configuration surface while keeping I/O implementations separate."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "BaseClient must contain zero I/O methods and only hold I/O-free configuration and state."
    RATIONALE "Hoisting logic into BaseClient is only viable for I/O-free logic; mixing sync/async I/O there breaks the parallel design."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Client must not await internally and AsyncClient must not block internally; such shortcuts are bugs, not optimizations."
    RATIONALE "Each class must be purely sync or purely async; mixing breaks the design."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Docstring parity between Client and AsyncClient is a maintenance requirement, not a style preference."
    RATIONALE "Identical API surface across both classes is load-bearing for documentation and user affordance."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Every I/O method (send, get, post, put, delete, patch, head, options, stream, request) is implemented twice — once on Client (sync) and once on AsyncClient (async) — with parallel signatures and identical semantics."
    RATIONALE "Type safety and clear sync vs async separation require duplicated I/O methods."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Resource management is per-subclass: Client uses __enter__/__exit__ and AsyncClient uses __aenter__/__aexit__; wrong context manager shape raises at entry."
    RATIONALE "Prevents accidental misuse of sync/async context management."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Sync vs async choice is made at instantiation time, not at call time; a given instance is never mixed-mode."
    RATIONALE "Keeps return types statically resolvable and avoids runtime dispatch complexity."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Tests must cover both sync and async paths; the test suite mirrors the parallel structure with paired sync/async test modules."
    RATIONALE "A fix verified only against Client can silently diverge on AsyncClient."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT sentinel distinguishes 'use client-configured default' from 'explicitly disable' (None); both clients use it across I/O method signatures."
    RATIONALE "Provides unambiguous semantics for per-request overrides since Python lacks a way to omit parameters callee-visibly."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "httpx provides two parallel concrete client classes (sync Client and async AsyncClient) instead of a single polymorphic client or sync-only with adapter."
    RATIONALE "Python lacks native async/sync polymorphism; parallel classes preserve type inference and make the sync/async choice explicit at instantiation."
  INTENT ADR-03 hard "Client features (redirects, auth flows, cookie persistence, event hooks, timeout coordination) live above the transport layer and must not be reimplemented in transports"
    RATIONALE "Separation of concerns: transports handle request-to-response, clients handle higher-level orchestration"
  INTENT ADR-03 hard "Clients delegate their __enter__/__exit__ to the underlying transports"
    RATIONALE "Ensures proper resource cleanup propagates through the transport layer"
  INTENT ADR-03 hard "Transport is a first-class pluggable abstraction with two parallel abstract base classes (sync and async)"
    RATIONALE "Enables swappable transports for testing, in-process ASGI/WSGI, custom pooling, and per-URL routing without monkey-patching"
  INTENT ADR-01 soft "Users should omit parameters rather than explicitly passing USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT; None is reserved for explicit disable."
    RATIONALE "USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT is an internal implementation detail exposed only out of necessity."
  INTENT docstring:httpx/_auth.py soft "If the auth scheme performs I/O or uses synchronization primitives, override sync_auth_flow() and/or async_auth_flow() instead of auth_flow() to provide specialized implementations used by Client and AsyncClient."
    RATIONALE "Specialized sync/async implementations are needed when the auth flow blocks or uses locks, so Client and AsyncClient can use the appropriate variant."
  INTENT docstring:httpx/_auth.py soft "When the auth scheme performs I/O or uses synchronization primitives, override sync_auth_flow() and/or async_auth_flow() instead of auth_flow() so Client and AsyncClient use specialized implementations."
    RATIONALE "I/O or locking in a generic auth_flow could block or misbehave; specialized sync/async variants are recommended."
  INTENT docstring:httpx/_transports/base.py soft "Developers should typically use the Client class higher-level API rather than calling this transport API directly"
    RATION
